generated_for_sub_pass: §1.2 Supabase Implementation 0.2 — Dependency and Public Config Injection Decision

selected_dependency_strategy.name: deferred_version_pinned_browser_esm_import
rationale: The current Direct UI Membrane is a static GitHub Pages preview workspace with no package manifest, lockfile, or build step.
A future version-pinned browser ESM import can load the Supabase browser library without adding package files or changing repository architecture.
No dependency URL, import statement, package file, install command, or live client initialization is added in this pass.
package_strategy_boundary: A package-based Supabase dependency remains a future architecture change only.
Adding package.json, a lockfile, or npm installation requires separate Operator authorization.
Package installation is not authorized by this decision pass.

selected_public_config_injection_strateg.name: deferred_generated_runtime_public_config_object
selected_public_config_injection_strateg.future_browser_config_source: A deployment or operator-controlled runtime config object may be generated outside committed source and loaded before js/supabase-client.js.

supabase_url_delivery.name: SUPABASE_URL
supabase_url_delivery.future_delivery: Read from the generated runtime public config object after deployment/operator injection.

supabase_publishable_key_delivery.name: SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
supabase_publishable_key_delivery.future_delivery: Read from the generated runtime public config object after deployment/operator injection.

supabase_anon_key_legacy_fallback.name: SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
supabase_anon_key_legacy_fallback.future_delivery: May be read from the same generated runtime public config object when SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY is absent.

absent_config_behavior_before_live_initi: js/supabase-client.js may continue to resolve absent_config without network calls.
Absent public config must keep live client initialization unauthorized.
No UI may collect credentials as a substitute for missing public config.

secret_boundary.service_role_key_boundary: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY remains server-secret only and must not appear in browser/client implementation files.
secret_boundary.jwt_secret_boundary: SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET remains forbidden in browser/client files.
secret_boundary.bounded_reference_policy: Server-secret names and forbidden string patterns may appear only in bounded validators and data records that name them as forbidden terms.

next_threshold.name: §1.2 Supabase Implementation 0.3 — Live Client Initialization
may_initialize_live_client_only_after: version-pinned browser ESM dependency loading is explicitly represented in the authorized client file
runtime public config injection is represented safely without committed real, fake, or placeholder values
SUPABASE_URL is available from the approved public config source
SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY is available from the approved public config source, or SUPABASE_ANON_KEY is available as a legacy-compatible fallback
server-secret names and forbidden string patterns remain absent from browser/client implementation files except bounded forbidden-term validators or records
package installation remains unperformed unless separately authorized by the Operator
next_threshold.explicit_authorization_boundary: 0.3 may initialize a live Supabase browser client only after the selected dependency and public config strategy is satisfied, and still without login UI unless separately authorized.

claimable_after_this_pass: Supabase dependency and public config injection decision exists
future dependency loading strategy is selected for the static no-build architecture
future browser-safe public config injection strategy is selected without committed values
live Supabase client initialization remains deferred
not_claimable_after_this_pass: real Supabase config exists in committed files

smallest_truthful_next_commit.name: §1.2 Supabase Implementation 0.3 — Live Client Initialization
smallest_truthful_next_commit.purpose: Add the authorized static dependency loading and runtime public config wiring needed to initialize only the browser client, without login UI or credential capture.
